source.title: Adaptive self-regulation of unattainable goals: goal disengagement, goal reengagement, and subjective well-being

confidence_rationale: Establishes that the adaptive-disengagement trigger is an unattainability APPRAISAL (not discouragement, not an evidence counter) and that disengagement is only adaptive when paired with reengagement. Directly supports the ARC-078 abandon-trigger design. Correlational individual-difference construct (Goal Adjustment Scale), so the trigger's computation is assumed-upstream, not modelled -- which is exactly the function REE must implement.
failure_signatures: Too late: difficulty disengaging from unattainable goals -> sustained distress, intrusive goal-related thought, lower well-being.
Too early / unconditional: disengaging from still-attainable goals -> loss of beneficial persistence.
Disengage-without-reengage: distress relieved but well-being not restored -- abandonment uncoupled from redirection leaves a purpose vacuum.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Goal disengagement (withdrawing effort/commitment from an unattainable goal) and goal reengagement (committing to new goals) are separable capacities both positively associated with subjective well-being; disengagement is adaptive specifically when a goal is appraised as unattainable, and is beneficial only when coupled with reengagement.
mapping.ree_translation: The ARC-078 C3 abandon trigger should be gated by an unattainability APPRAISAL of the target goal, not by accumulated failure/effort. And abandonment must be architecturally coupled to redirection (a reengagement step), not implemented as a bare delete -- disengage-without-reengage is itself a documented failure mode.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Unattainability is treated as a given upstream input -- the paper specifies THAT it triggers disengagement, not HOW the system computes it; that computation is precisely what a REE abandon trigger must supply, so this constrains the gate's input semantics but not its mechanism. Cross-sectional self-report.
mapping.source_context: Three human samples (115 undergraduates; 120 young/older adults; 45 parents incl. parents of children with cancer); Goal Adjustment Scale; subjective well-being outcomes.

confidence_components.notes: Good source quality (multi-sample incl. a clinical-stress sample; widely-used construct). Mapping fidelity moderate-high: the unattainability-appraisal trigger and the disengage+reengage coupling map cleanly. Transfer risk moderate (correlational self-report -> architectural trigger).
